message:
test(fnp-python): shape + axis edge parity probe on reductions

Adds `shape_and_axis_edge_parity_across_reductions` — targeted probe
for dimension-bookkeeping bugs in cod's rust-ported reductions + k74v.8
passthroughs. Shape-preservation bugs are among the most invisible
because the numerical value is correct but a downstream matmul /
broadcast silently fails because keepdims dropped or added the wrong
axis.

Coverage: 13 reduction ops × 5 axis-edge cases = ~65 instances, each
comparing both SHAPE and VALUE against numpy for a 3-D (2, 2, 3) input.

Ops tested:
- k74v.8 passthrough: sum, prod, mean, std, var, amax, amin, max, min,
                      all, any, argmax, argmin

Axis-edge cases probed:
1. axis=-1       (last-axis, negative wrap-around)
2. axis=(0,)     (single-element tuple — equivalent to axis=0)
3. axis=(0, 2)   (multi-axis tuple on 3-D, reduces along both)
4. axis=0, keepdims=True   (dim preserved as length-1)
5. axis=None, keepdims=True (full reduction → (1,1,1) result)

Each case:
  a. checks result SHAPE parity (vec<usize> equality)
  b. checks result VALUE parity via numpy.allclose(equal_nan=True)
  c. checks success-surface parity (both accept or both reject — some
     reductions like argmax don't take tuple axis)

RESULT: zero divergences across all ~65 cases. Shape preservation,
negative-axis wrap-around, tuple-axis multi-reduction, and keepdims
handling all match numpy exactly.

FIFTH consecutive review cycle returning zero HIGH/CRITICAL divergences:
  Cycle 4: 8 adversarial error-surface cases
  Cycle 5: 26 adversarial + axis-OOB
  Cycle 6: 9 metamorphic algebraic invariants
  Cycle 7: ~70 dtype-promotion (fn, dtype) pairs
  Cycle 8: ~65 shape/axis edges (this commit)

Total probe coverage: ~178 cases across 5 independent conformance
dimensions, no divergences found.

Verified via:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib shape_and_axis_edge_parity
1 passed, 0 failed, finished in 0.15s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
